Consumer Price Index for All Urban Consumers: All Items in U.S. City Average (CPIAUCSL)

Source: U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics

Release: Consumer Price Index

Units: Index 1982-1984=100, Seasonally Adjusted

Frequency: Monthly

Latest observation: January 2026, value 319.082

The Consumer Price Index for All Urban Consumers: All Items in U.S. City Average was 319.082 in January 2026, representing a 12-month change of 2.9 percent.

The annual inflation rate slowed from 3.1% in December 2025 to 2.9% in January 2026, slightly below economists' expectations of 3.0%.

Core CPI, which excludes food and energy, rose 3.2% year-over-year in January, compared to 3.3% in December.

FRED Series ID: CPIAUCSL. Seasonal Adjustment: Seasonally Adjusted.
